query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "the mirage of optimizing training policies: monotonic inference polici" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it investigates the same core mechanism of "inference policies" for LLMs (specifically comparing iterative Gibbs sampling against standard autoregressive generation) and offers a foundational methodological alternative to the "monotonic" policies critiqued in the user's referenced work. It provides the necessary canonical prior work on structured inference strategies that a literature review on optimizing LLM training/inference policies would require.
